HSC Survey Results

Scots Bay Survey # 11 Results from 21/09/2025

Survey Details

2 commercial purse seiners conducted this fishing survey on 21/09/2025 starting at 21:00:00, which was 3.85 hours before hide tide.

All vessels ran two transects each in the Main Survey Box.

There was no plankton tow or CTD cast conducted.

No tags were applied during this survey.

Samples were obtained for target strength estimation.

Tides Tables

Tide Schedule for Margaretsville NS, Station #315m Source: www.waterlevels.gc.ca

Acoustic Equipment

Each vessel was equipped with a Simrad ES38-18/200-18C combi (2-in-1) transducer prior to the survey that contains a 38kHz split beam and 200 kHz single beam transducer. Data was logged to the computer hard-drive for post-processing. All participating boats were calibrated on 38kHz before the survey.

Survey Results

Main Results

The results are subject to change as DFO will re-evaluate data editing, area estimation and apply a target strength.

Preliminary biomass prediction for Scots Bay using standardized parallel transects is 11,201mt, and with turnover applied 10,471mt.

Vessel Performance

Speed

Average vessel speed was 8.38. This is above the 8 knot requirement; vessels are urged to slow down to prevent backscatter noise from interfering with the acoustic data.

The speed for each transect are rudimentary and are calculated based on distance. If a significant amount of distance is cut, the speed will appear slower.

Distance

More than 2km of distance was cut from the planned lines; vessels are reminded to complete their assigned lines straight and without deviating or cutting corners short.
